paper.title: Hypervelocity star candidates from Gaia DR2 and DR3 proper motions and parallaxes

review.summary: This paper selects and characterises hypervelocity-star candidates from Gaia DR2 and DR3 using proper motions and parallaxes, with a focus on 72 nearest extreme candidates. Internal assets comprise a schema-description table of quality criteria, a primary object catalog of the 72 candidates with astrometry and computed velocities, and a companion observation-log-style table with next-neighbour and IPD diagnostics. External resources include the IRSA Finder Chart, the Gaia science-performance page, an ASAS-SN variable-star entry, a Gaia technical note on RUWE, and a CDS data-availability statement. Generic database acknowledgements and citation-helper macros have been removed.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Documents the DR3 quality flags, astrometric parameters, and critical thresholds used to vet the 72 nearest extreme HVS candidates, serving as a reproducibility checklist.

source_refs.caption: DR3 quality flags and parameters used for HVS candidates, in particular for the 72 nearest extreme candidates studied in Sect.~\ref{Sect_nearextrHVS}
source_refs.label: Tab_quali

columns.name: Quantity/ | criterion
columns.meaning: Quantity or selection criterion name

columns.name: DR3 column
columns.meaning: Corresponding Gaia DR3 database column name

columns.name: Critical values | (? among 72)
columns.meaning: Critical value or range applied to the candidate sample, with the count among the 72 candidates in parentheses

columns.name: Remark
columns.meaning: Remark on the criterion (e.g. high/low priority, section reference)

internal_tables.evidence: DR3 quality flags and parameters used for HVS candidates, in particular for the 72 nearest extreme candidates studied in Sect.~\ref{Sect_nearextrHVS}

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Primary catalog of the 72 nearest extreme HVS candidates selected from Gaia DR3, listing basic astrometry, photometry, proper motions, quality indicators, and computed tangential velocities.

source_refs.caption: Basic DR3 data on 72 nearest extreme HVS candidates with high ($RPlx$$>$5) and low (3$<$$RPlx$$<$5) priority, sorted by $G$ magnitude
source_refs.label: Tab_72dr3

columns.name: No.
columns.meaning: Running number in the candidate list

columns.name: Gaia DR3
columns.meaning: Gaia DR3 source identifier

columns.name: G | [mag]
columns.meaning: Gaia G-band apparent magnitude

columns.name: Nper
columns.meaning: Number of visibility periods used in the astrometric solution

columns.name: Plx | [mas]
columns.meaning: Parallax

columns.name: RPlx
columns.meaning: Parallax over error (significance)

columns.name: pmRA | [mas/yr]
columns.meaning: Proper motion in right ascension (cos-dec corrected)

columns.name: pmDE | [mas/yr]
columns.meaning: Proper motion in declination

columns.name: gofAL
columns.meaning: Astrometric goodness-of-fit in the along-scan direction

columns.name: epsi | [mas]
columns.meaning: Astrometric excess noise

columns.name: sepsi
columns.meaning: Significance of the astrometric excess noise

columns.name: amax | [mas]
columns.meaning: Semi-major axis of the astrometric error ellipse

columns.name: IPDgofha
columns.meaning: IPD goodness-of-fit in the along-scan direction

columns.name: RUWE
columns.meaning: Renormalised unit weight error

columns.name: G_abs | [mag]
columns.meaning: Absolute G magnitude derived from parallax

columns.name: G-RP | [mag]
columns.meaning: Gaia G-RP colour index

columns.name: RV | [km/s]
columns.meaning: Heliocentric radial velocity (where available)

columns.name: vtan | [km/s]
columns.meaning: Heliocentric tangential velocity

columns.name: vtan_g | [km/s]
columns.meaning: Galactocentric tangential velocity

internal_tables.evidence: Basic DR3 data on 72 nearest extreme HVS candidates with high ($RPlx$$>$5) and low (3$<$$RPlx$$<$5) priority, sorted by $G$ magnitude

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Companion table to Tab_72dr3 providing next-neighbour search results, IPD parameters, duplication and solution flags, and local-plus-global parameter comparison ratios for each of the 72 HVS candidates.

source_refs.caption: DR3 next neighbours, IPD parameters, flags, and results from local$+$global parameter comparison of 72 HVS candidates
source_refs.label: Tab_72nnflg

columns.name: No.
columns.meaning: Running number

columns.name: G | [mag]
columns.meaning: Gaia G-band magnitude

columns.name: Gnn | [mag]
columns.meaning: G magnitude of the closest next neighbour

columns.name: dnn | [arc | sec]
columns.meaning: Angular separation to the closest next neighbour

columns.name: n5as
columns.meaning: Number of DR3 sources within 5 arcsec

columns.name: IPD | fmp
columns.meaning: IPD fraction of multi-peak

columns.name: IPD | fow
columns.meaning: IPD fraction of odd windows

columns.name: Sol | ved
columns.meaning: Astrometric solution type (number of parameters solved)

columns.name: Dup
columns.meaning: Duplicated source flag

columns.name: Nper | l\Delta g\Delta
columns.meaning: Visibility periods used, with local and global delta flags

columns.name: e_Plx | lR gR
columns.meaning: Parallax error, local and global rank ratios

columns.name: e_pmRA | lR gR
columns.meaning: Proper motion RA error, local and global rank ratios

columns.name: e_pmDE | lR gR
columns.meaning: Proper motion Dec error, local and global rank ratios

columns.name: gofAL | lR gR
columns.meaning: Astrometric gofAL, local and global rank ratios

columns.name: epsi | lR gR
columns.meaning: Astrometric excess noise, local and global rank ratios

columns.name: sepsi | lR gR
columns.meaning: Significance of excess noise, local and global rank ratios

columns.name: amax | lR gR
columns.meaning: Semi-major axis of error ellipse, local and global rank ratios

columns.name: IPDgofha | lR gR
columns.meaning: IPD gofAL, local and global rank ratios

columns.name: RUWE | lR gR
columns.meaning: RUWE, local and global rank ratios

columns.name: RPlx
columns.meaning: Parallax over error

external_resources.description: ASAS-SN variable-star database entry for a candidate showing 0.5-day periodicity, used to identify a probable RR Lyrae contaminant.

source_refs.context:
\citepads[ASAS-SN;][]{2018MNRAS.477.3145J} % Jayasinghe+others ASAS-SN v. stars
catalogue of variable stars provides a period of about 
0.5 days\footnote{https://asas-sn.osu.edu/variables/107503}.
Its variability was also mentioned and classified in DR2, according to which
it belongs to the class of fundamental-mode RR Lyrae stars, but not in DR3.

external_resources.description: The paper states that the combined data from Tables Tab_72dr3 and Tab_72nnflg will be made available in electronic form at the CDS.
Duplicate mention of CDS data availability for the combined candidate tables.
